input: FINDINGS: Evaluation of the lung parenchyma is somewhat limited by significant respiratory motion. The lung bases are clear without focal consolidation or pleural effusion. There are multiple ill-defined, calcified subcarinal lymph nodes, and a small calcified pulmonary nodule, consistent with prior granulomatous disease. The visualized portion of the heart is normal in size without pericardial effusion. The liver appears diffusely low in attenuation, but is equivocal for fatty liver disease based on Hounsfield units in comparison with the spleen. The left lobe of the liver appears mildly enlarged. input: FINDINGS: There is a stable 4-mm pulmonary nodule seen within the posterior left lower lobe (image 17 series 4, when compared to CT of 11-23-2006. No new pulmonary nodules seen. No pleural effusion. The heart size is within normal limits. No pericardial effusion is noted. Triple vessel coronary calcifications are noted. The patient has had prior median sternotomy for CABG. The liver, spleen, pancreas, adrenal glands are within normal limits. Radiopaque gallstones are noted. No gallbladder wall thickening or pericholecystic fluid. No intra-or extrahepatic biliary dilatation. There has been interval development of bilateral hydroureteronephrosis since the prior CT of 01/12/09, moderate on the right and mild on the left, extending to the level of the ileal pouch. No perinephric stranding. No renal or ureteral calculi noted. There has been interval placement of a percutaneous drainage catheter into the large fluid collection seen within the parastomal hernia sac along the anterior abdominal wall on the CT 1/12/2009, with near complete resolution of the fluid within this region. A right lower quadrant parastomal hernia containing loops of distal small bowel again noted. There has been interval development of marked dilatation of the small bowel upstream to the segment that is contained within the parastomal hernia, maximal caliber of 5 cm. A transition point is noted within the hernia sac, with more distal ileum decompressed. There is minimal stranding of the mesenteric fat adjacent to small bowel loops within the hernia sac, without extraluminal gas or mesenteric fluid. Stool and gas is noted within the colon up to the level of the rectum. No free fluid or free gas. The patient is again noted to be status post cystectomy and bilateral pelvic lymphadenectomies. The patient is also status post hysterectomy. Extensive mural calcifications are seen along the abdominal aorta without evidence for aneurysm. No acute fracture. Degenerative changes are seen throughout the visualized thoracolumbar spine. No suspicious bony lesion is identified. There is stable pars interarticularis defect at right L5 with mild L5 on S1 anterolisthesis. IMPRESSION: 1. INTERVAL DEVELOPMENT OF HIGH-GRADE SMALL BOWEL OBSTRUCTION WITH A TRANSITION POINT SEEN WITHIN THE PARASTOMAL HERNIA SAC AND UPSTREAM SMALL BOWEL DILATATION TO 5-CM IN CALIBER, WHEN COMPARED TO PRIOR CT OF January 12, 09.2. NEAR COMPLETE INTERVAL RESOLUTION OF FLUID COLLECTION WITHIN THE PARASTOMAL HERNIA SAC, WITH A PIGTAIL DRAINAGE EXTENDING INTO THIS REGION. 3. INTERVAL DEVELOPMENT OF BILATERAL HYDRONEPHROSIS, MODERATE ON THE RIGHT AND MILD ON THE LEFT. 4. CHOLELITHIASIS WITHOUT EVIDENCE FOR ACUTE CHOLECYSTITIS. FINDINGS WERE DISCUSSED WITH MEYERS DAVID R AT 1:05 A. M. ON 2-25-2009 SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: Dependent consolidation within the bilateral lung bases likely represents atelectasis. An infection cannot be excluded. There are no pleural effusions. The visualized heart and thoracic vasculature are unremarkable. The liver is fatty and otherwise unremarkable. This patient is status post cholecystectomy. The kidneys, adrenal glands, spleen, pancreas, and visualized vasculature are unremarkable. There is a Foley catheter within an air and fluid-filled urinary bladder. No abnormal abdominal or pelvic adenopathy is identified. Extensive postsurgical changes are present, consistent with recent colectomy, ileoanal J-pouch construction, as well as diverting loop ileostomy. There is a small to moderate amount of free fluid, predominantly perihepatic. A moderate amount of intraperitoneal free air is also noted mostly anterior to the liver, slightly greater than expected, though likely related to postoperative status. There are numerous loops of mildly dilated small bowel, predominantly fluid filled. This mild dilatation extends to the loop ileostomy. The small bowel loops distal to the ileostomy are decompressed. These findings may be related to a postoperative ileus, given the recent surgery. However, a partial obstruction at the ileostomy site is also possible. The visualized small bowel otherwise demonstrates normal enhancing mucosa without pneumatosis. There is no intraabdominal abscess. The anastomotic site appears patent. A percutaneous pelvic drainage catheter is noted. A T12 vertebral body hemangioma is noted, as well as sacroiliac joint sclerosis. IMPRESSION: 1. POSTSURGICAL CHANGES IN A PATIENT POSTOPERATIVE DAY # 3, STATUS POST COLECTOMY WITH ILEOANAL J-POUCH CONSTRUCTION AND DIVERTING LOOP ILEOSTOMY. SMALL TO MODERATE FREE FLUID AND FREE AIR IS SLIGHTLY GREATER THAN EXPECTED, THOUGH LIKELY ATTRIBUTABLE TO POSTOPERATIVE STATUS. MILDLY DILATED LOOPS OF SMALL BOWEL UP TO LOOP ILEOSTOMY SITE COULD BE RELATED TO POSTOPERATIVE ILEUS, THOUGH PARTIAL FUNCTIONAL SMALL BOWEL OBSTRUCTION AT ILEOSTOMY SITE IS ALSO POSSIBLE. NO INTRAABDOMINAL ABSCESS OR ANASTOMOTIC LEAK. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDINGS, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

related: small bowel loops distal to ileostomy (location:present)

input: FINDINGS: ABDOMEN / PELVIS: Interval placement of a drain entering from the right upper anterior abdomen, with its tip pigtailed within a hepatic subcapsular rim-enhancing fluid collection containing a few foci of air. This collection has decreased significantly in size since the prior exam, measuring approximately 6.9 x 4.7 x 2.5 cm, previously measuring approximately 18.1 x 7.1 x 8.1 cm. There has also been significant interval decrease in size of a rim-enhancing fluid collection previously seen just superior to the coloenteric anastomosis within the right upper abdominal quadrant. There is a small persistent rim-enhancing fluid collection measuring 1.9 x 1.2 cm (series 2, image 50). No new fluid collection is demonstrated. There is gallbladder wall thickening, likely reactive and mildly improved since the prior exam. No radiopaque gallstones demonstrated. No significant biliary ductal dilatation. The liver is unremarkable. The pancreas, spleen, adrenal glands and kidneys are unremarkable. Colonic diverticulosis. No free intraperitoneal fluid. No adenopathy by size criteria. CHEST: Interval removal of a right pleural drain with interval increase in a small right pleural effusion. Right lower lobe atelectasis. Stable small left pleural effusion with left lower lobe atelectasis. A 2-mm calcified granuloma within the left lower lobe. BONES: Degenerative changes. No suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesions. Subcutaneous edema within the right lateral abdomen. IMPRESSION: 1. INTERVAL PLACEMENT OF A DRAIN WITHIN A HEPATIC SUBCAPSULAR RIM-ENHANCING FLUID COLLECTION, WHICH HAS SIGNIFICANTLY DECREASED IN SIZE SINCE THE PRIOR EXAM, NOW MEASURING APPROXIMATELY 4.7 X 2.5 X 7.0 CM. SIMILARLY, INTERVAL DECREASE IN SIZE OF A RIM-ENHANCING FLUID COLLECTION PREVIOUSLY SEEN JUST SUPERIOR TO THE COLOENTERIC ANASTOMOSIS WITHIN THE RIGHT UPPER ABDOMINAL QUADRANT. THERE IS A PERSISTENT 1.9 X 1.2 CM RIM-ENHANCING FLUID COLLECTION. 2. INTERVAL REMOVAL OF A RIGHT PLEURAL DRAIN AND INTERVAL INCREASE IN SIZE OF A SMALL RIGHT PLEURAL EFFUSION. STABLE SMALL LEFT PLEURAL EFFUSION. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDINGS, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: VASCULATURE: The heart size is normal without a pericardial effusion. The aorta is normal in course and caliber. The main pulmonary artery is normal in course and caliber. While not a dedicated pulmonary embolism study, no filling defects are seen in the main or lobar pulmonary arteries to suggest pulmonary embolism. The SMA and IMA are unremarkable. The bilateral renal arteries are normal. There are bilateral inferior accessory renal arteries. The celiac trunk again has a dissection which extends into the proper hepatic and proximal splenic arteries. The splenic artery supplied by the true lumen while the common hepatic artery supplied by the false lumen. There is a replaced right hepatic artery. Soft tissue thickening is seen around the vessel, particularly around the proximal common hepatic artery. This thickening is similar in appearance to the prior study. LOWER CHEST: Minimal dependent atelectasis. Otherwise the visualized portions of the lung bases and heart are within normal limits. ABDOMEN: The liver and gallbladder are normal in appearance, without evidence of periportal edema or intrahepatic biliary ductal dilation. input: FINDINGS: The lung bases demonstrate a random distribution of innumerable pulmonary nodules, the largest of which measures 11 mm and is seen within the lingula (series 4, image 7) with a right pleural effusion and adjacent right basilar atelectasis. The heart is normal in appearance, central venous catheter tip can be seen at the cavoatrial junction. The distal esophagus is significant for numerous esophageal varices. The stomach is dilated, but is otherwise unremarkable. The small bowel is diffusely thickened, and enhances normally. The colon is normal in appearance. There is a large amount of abdominal ascites. The liver demonstrates intrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation. A hypodense lesion within the right hepatic lobe (series 4, image 99) measures 1.6 cm. Another similarly sized hypodense liver lesion is present within the caudate lobe. A hypervascular focus within segment II likely represents a hemangioma (series 4, image 46). There is occlusion of the portal vein, with cavernous transformation. The hepatic veins appear patent. A 3.6-cm rounded mass within the pancreatic neck invades the hepatic hilum, and encases the hepatic artery and a splenic artery and the gastroduodenal artery is not seen. The superior mesenteric vein, and splenic vein are occluded in the region of this mass, and there are multiple mesenteric collaterals. This same mass abuts the duodenum. The spleen multiple rounded hypodensities in the spleen (series 4, image 81 and 134). There is moderate intrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation that is most prominent centrally, though the gallbladder contains no radio-opaque stones. There is minimal pericholecystic fluid. The adrenal glands are unremarkable. The kidneys demonstrate normal contrast enhancement and excretion. The bladder is incompletely distended but appears normal. The pelvic organs are unremarkable. The abdominal aorta is normal in appearance, the IVC is patent. The retroperitoneum and mesentery show no pathologically enlarged lymph nodes. The osseous structures are unremarkable without acute bony or joint abnormalities. IMPRESSION: 1. LOCALLY AND DISTALLY ADVANCED PANCREATIC CARCINOMA OF THE PANCREATIC NECK WITH HEPATIC, PULMONARY, AND LIKELY PERITONEAL METASTASES. THERE IS ENCASEMENT OF THE CELIAC AXIS, OCCLUSION OF THE SPLENIC, SUPERIOR MESENTERIC VEINS AND MAIN PORTAL VEIN WITH CAVERNOUS TRANSFORMATION, AND EXTENSION OF THE TUMOR INTO THE PORTA HEPATIS WITH OBSTRUCTION OF THE CENTRAL BILIARY TREE AND CONSECUTIVE INTRAHEPATIC BILIARY DILATATION AND INVASION INTO THE DESCENDING DUODENUM (ACCORDING TO RECENT OUTSIDE UPPER GI SERIES NO EVIDENCE FOR OUTLET OBASTRUCTION). 2. LARGE AMOUNT OF INTRA-ABDOMINAL ASCITES. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber and contour. The principal vascular structures of the abdomen are patent. No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes of the retroperitoneum, mesentery, pelvic side walls or inguinal regions. Few sclerotic lesions most likely representative of bone islands in the left hip, left iliac bone, and the right sixth rib. No suspicious osseous lesions. IMPRESSION: 1. input: FINDINGS: The cardiac silhouette remains mildly enlarged. Coronary artery atherosclerotic disease is present. No pericardial effusion. The distal descending thoracic aorta is normal in course and caliber. The distal esophagus and gastroesophageal junction are grossly unremarkable. No pleural effusions. Evaluation of the lung parenchyma demonstrates dependent, reticular and ground glass opacities compatible with atelectasis. The liver is normal in size, contour and attenuation. No focal hepatic mass. Numerous well-circumscribed hypoattenuating lesions are again noted scattered throughout the liver parenchyma series 3, image 72, 90, 93, 156 and 168, which are stable in size and distribution but remain too small to characterize. No intrahepatic biliary dilatation. The hepatic and portal veins are patent. The gallbladder is unremarkable without evidence of radiopaque gallstones, gallbladder wall thickening or pericholecystic fluid. The adrenal glands are within normal limits. The spleen is normal in size, contour and attenuation. The pancreas is within normal limits without evidence of mass or ductal dilatation. The kidneys are symmetric in size, contour, contrast enhancement and excretion. Previously described heterogeneous left renal perfusion on the delayed images is less apparent than on prior exams. No urothelial enhancement or perinephric fluid collections. No focal renal mass, hydronephrosis or nephrolithiasis. The bladder is grossly unremarkable. The prostate contains multiple brachytherapy beads. The seminal vesicles are not definitely identified. No free fluid within the abdomen or pelvis. The stomach is grossly unremarkable. The small bowel is within normal limits without evidence of bowel wall thickening, hyperemia, pneumatosis or obstruction. The patient is status post abdominal peroneal resection with a left lower quadrant colostomy in place. Scattered diverticula are identified without evidence of diverticulitis. The appendix is within normal limits. No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes within the pelvis. Subcentimeter mesenteric lymph nodes are again identified. Evaluation of the right perineum demonstrates new abnormal, mildly lobulated soft tissue measuring 5.8 x 3.3 cm by at least 5.4 cm in cranial caudal diameter extending from the anus to the right thigh adductor musculature series 4, image 66 and series 3, image 492. The abdominal aorta is normal in course and caliber and demonstrates atherosclerotic disease. No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes within the abdomen or pelvis. Evaluation of the visualized osseous structures demonstrates straightening of the normal lumbar lordosis and multilevel degenerative changes. There is a stable compression deformity of the T12 vertebral body of less than 25%. No new compression deformities are identified. Left L5-S1 laminectomy defects are again identified. Addendum Begins NEW, LOBULATED RIGHT PERINEAL SOFT TISSUE EXTENDING FROM THE ANUS TO THE RIGHT THIGH ADDUCTOR MUSCULATURE CORRESPONDS TO A GRACILIS FLAP. THIS ADDENDUM FOLLOWS A DISCUSSION BETWEEN MD Lyric AND Fernandez Hansika V. Addendum Ends IMPRESSION: 1. STATUS POST PRIOR ABDOMINOPERINEAL RESECTION AND LEFT LOWER QUADRANT COLOSTOMY. NEW LOBULATED RIGHT PERINEAL SOFT TISSUE EXTENDING FROM THE ANUS TO THE RIGHT THIGH ADDUCTOR MUSCULATURE. ALTHOUGH FINDINGS MAY REPRESENT THE SEQUELA OF A CHRONIC INFLAMMATORY PROCESS, LOCAL DISEASE RECURRENCE CANNOT BE EXCLUDED. RECOMMEND FURTHER EVALUATION WITH PET CT. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLY SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: Limited evaluation of the chest demonstrates clear lung bases. Heart size is within normal limits and there is no evidence of pericardial or pleural effusion. Stable appearance of a large hiatal hernia. In the abdomen, the liver is unremarkable without evidence of focal hepatic lesion. There is mild to moderate intrahepatic biliary dilatation. The common bile duct measures approximately 1.4 cm in maximal diameter. The patient is status post cholecystectomy. The spleen, pancreas, adrenal glands, and kidneys are grossly unremarkable. Two midline ventral hernias are again seen. The superior hernia was demonstrated to contain only fat on the prior examination, but now contains one wall of the transverse colon, consistent with a Richter's hernia. There is no evidence of bowel obstruction. There is no bowel wall thickening. The inferior hernia is fat containing just superior to the umbilicus and appears unchanged. input: FINDINGS: There is redemonstration of a grossly stable approximately11 x 9 x 6 cm pelvic fluid collection, which is primary located anterior to the rectum, but with a small continuous segment that wraps around the left lateral portion of the rectum to the presacral area. In addition, there is extraluminal contrast identified within this collection, with a possible fistulous connection to adjacent loops of small bowel at the superior aspect of this collection, best seen on series 545, image 104 and series 544, image 65). In addition, there are two pelvic pigtail catheters terminating within this collection. Patient is status post bladder resection with an ileal conduit in place. Post surgical changes again seen in the pelvis and lower abdomen, including a double barrel left-sided colostomy. There is no evidence of small bowel obstruction seen. Contrast material is seen within the distal small bowel and descending sigmoid and rectum. There is redemonstration of multiple prominent subcentimeter retroperitoneal, para-aortic, iliac, and mesenteric lymph nodes, which are not changed from the prior study. There is mild intrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation, unchanged in the prior study dated 03-17-03. The liver is otherwise unremarkable without evidence of fatty change or masses seen. There is redemonstration of a peripherally located wedge shaped hypodense lesion within the posterior aspect of the the inferior right hepatic lobe (segment 6), that measures approximately 11 x 9 mm., stable from the prior study. There is no extrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation. The hepatic and portal veins are patent. The gallbladder has no radio-opaque lesions. The pancreas is unremarkable. The spleen is normal. The adrenal glands show normal appearance. There is moderate right hydronephrosis and proximal right hydroureter, unchanged on the prior study. No renal masses lesions seen bilaterally. There is mild left hydronephrosis. Layering high density material is again seen in bilateral kidneys, may represent milk of calcium, unchanged from the prior study. There is redemonstration of marked irregularity and erosive changes of the left femoral head and neck and acetabulum with subluxation, with a multiloculated joint effusion, not significantly changed from the prior study. In addition, there is moderate asymmetric thickening of the musculature surrounding the left hip, may represent associated myositis. In addition, there is redemonstration of bony remodeling and irregularity of the left superior ramus, with an adjacent medially located 1.8 cm calcified fragment. The abdominal aorta shows mild atherosclerotic disease. The IVC is patent. The lung bases demonstrate trace bilateral pleural effusions, which are new from the prior study and bilateral dependent atelectatic changes, which are increased from the prior study. There is also mild to moderate diffuse anasarca, with dependent subcutaneous edema, increased from the prior study. The heart appears normal. The distal esophagus is normal. IMPRESSION: 1. STABLE 11 CM PELVIC ABSCESS, NOW CONTAINING INTERNAL CONTRAST MATERIAL FROM A POSSIBLE FISTULOUS CONNECTION TO THE SUPERIORLY LOCATED ADJACENT SMALL BOWEL 2. REDEMONSTRATION OF MARKED IRREGULARITY AND EROSIVE CHANGES OF THE LEFT HIP, WITH A MULTI LOCATED JOINT FLUID COLLECTION, AGAIN HIGH SUGGESTIVE OF SEPTIC ARTHRITIS AND OSTEOMYELITIS. THERE IS ALSO MILD ASYMMETRIC THICKENING AND PROMINENCE OF THE LEFT ANTERIOR COMPARTMENT HIP MUSCULATURE, WHICH MAY REPRESENT AN UNDERLYING MYOSITIS. 3. REDEMONSTRATION OF BONY REMODELING AND IRREGULARITY OF THE LEFT SUPERIOR RAMUS, WITH AN ADJACENT MEDIALLY LOCATED 1.8 CM CALCIFIED FRAGMENT. FINDINGS MAY REPRESENT POST SURGICAL CHANGES, THOUGH NOT ENTIRELY EXCLUDING RESIDUAL TUMOR. ATTENTION ON FOLLOW UP STUDIES IS RECOMMENDED. 4. NEW TRACE BILATERAL PLEURAL EFFUSIONS AND INCREASED DIFFUSE ANASARCA. 5. STABLE MODERATE RIGHT AND MILD LEFT HYDRONEPHROSIS. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: Cardiomegaly is present. No pericardial effusion. The distal descending thoracic aorta is normal in course and caliber. The distal esophagus and gastroesophageal junction are grossly unremarkable. No pleural effusions. Evaluation of the lung parenchyma demonstrates minimal dependent, reticular and ground glass opacities compatible with atelectasis. There is a nodular opacity adjacent to the right or border though shown to be vascular and likely represents AVM. The liver is again seen to have a nodular contour consistent with cirrhosis. There are areas of hyperdensity within the liver consistent with multiple prior TACE procedures. Multiple embolization coils are present causing streak artifact, seen previously. The shunt from the IVC to the portal vein appear stable and patent. There is a 2.9-cm bulge in the contour of the right lobe of the liver that was seen previously it and likely represents a regenerating nodule. No intrahepatic biliary dilatation. There is a peri-portocaval density that likely represents a densely calcified lymph node. There is no evidence of new masses. The gallbladder has several radiopaque gallstones, without gallbladder wall thickening or pericholecystic fluid. The adrenal glands have a normal appearance. The spleen is normal in size and attenuation. The pancreas appears normal without evidence of mass or ductal dilatation. The kidneys are symmetric in size, contour, contrast enhancement and excretion. No focal renal mass, hydronephrosis or nephrolithiasis. Punctate hypo-densities within the renal parenchyma are too small to characterize, however statistically, likely represent renal cysts. The stomach is grossly unremarkable. The small bowel appears normal without evidence of bowel wall thickening, hyperemia, pneumatosis or obstruction. The colon is normal in course and caliber. Scattered diverticula are present without evidence of diverticulitis. The appendix is within normal limits. No free fluid within the abdomen. The abdominal aorta is normal in course and caliber. No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes within the abdomen or pelvis. Visualized osseous structures are grossly normal. Addendum Begins Addendum Ends Addendum Begins Exam code correction only. Corrected charges are under accession number 06 59 64 65 35 99 0 (CT Triphasic Liver Mass Protocol). Addendum Ends IMPRESSION: 1. COMPARED TO PRIOR STUDY OF 3-3-2010 THIS STUDY DEMONSTRATES STABLE APPEARANCE OF CIRRHOSIS WITH EVIDENCE OF PRIOR TACE PROCEDURES. THERE IS NO EVIDENCE OF NEW HEPATIC LESIONS, HOWEVER NODULARITY OF LIVER AND PRESENCE OF STREAK ARTIFACTS SLIGHTLY LIMITS EVALUATION. SUMMARY: 2-ABNORMAL, PREVIOUSLY REPORTED I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: The visualized lung bases demonstrate unchanged bronchiectasis, mucous plugging, and tree-in-bud opacities in the right lower lobe without pleural effusions. Visualized portions of the heart are normal in appearance and size without pericardial effusion. Numerous hypoattenuating lesions consistent with metastases are present throughout the liver, which appear stable to increased in size. For reference, a lesion in segment 6 measures 1.9 cm, previously measuring 1.6 cm (3/393). The largest lesion in segment 5/6 measuring 5.2 x 7.1 cm (94) appears unchanged. A 7-mm lesion in segment 7 (3/38) is not definitely visualized on the prior study. Diffuse mild hypoattenuation of the liver consistent with steatosis is unchanged. Multiple fiducials in the liver appear unchanged. A punctate calcific density in the pancreas appears unchanged (3/92). The pancreas demonstrates prominent dorsal drainage into the minor papilla; it is not clear if there is pancreas divisum. The pancreas otherwise appears unremarkable. The spleen and adrenal glands are normal in appearance. A 3 mm stone in the lower pole of the right kidney (3/90) and a 1 mm stone in the midpole of the left kidney (3/46) are unchanged. There is unchanged mild right renal cortical scarring. Multiple hypoattenuating lesions in the kidney remain too small to characterize but likely represent cysts. Gallbladder demonstrates interval decreased gallbladder wall thickening and decreased residual adjacent stranding and fluid around the gallbladder and duodenum (3/90) without evidence of gallstones. There is a small hiatal hernia that appears more prominent on the current exam. The small and large bowel are normal in course and caliber without evidence of obstruction. The appendix appears normal (301/58). The bladder appears unremarkable. Multiple calcifications within the uterus may be secondary to fibroids. No abdominal, pelvic, or inguinal lymphadenopathy identified. Evaluation of the osseous structures demonstrates an unchanged lytic lesion in the left proximal femoral head measuring 2.4 cm with cortical breakthrough (3/247). A transverse fracture through the left L5 transverse process is also unchanged. Cortical irregularity at the right femoral head neck junction (3/262) appears unchanged. Numerous other scattered lucent and sclerotic foci are grossly unchanged. IMPRESSION: 1. NO CT EVIDENCE OF BOWEL OBSTRUCTION OR ILEUS AS CLINICALLY QUERIED. 2. DECREASED MILD GALLBLADDER WALL THICKENING AND FLUID AROUND THE GALLBLADDER AND DUODENUM. FURTHER EVALUATION WITH RIGHT UPPER QUADRANT ULTRASOUND MAY BE CONSIDERED IF CLINICALLY INDICATED. 3. MULTIPLE LIVER METASTASES ARE MILDLY INCREASED IN SIZE AND NUMBER MEASURING UP TO 5.2 X 7.1 CM IN SEGMENT 5/6.4. UNCHANGED SCATTERED LYTIC AND SCLEROTIC OSSEOUS METASTASES, INCLUDING A LYTIC LESION IN THE LEFT PROXIMAL FEMUR THAT IS AT RISK OF PATHOLOGIC FRACTURE. 5. BIBASILAR BRONCHIECTASIS, MUCOUS PLUGGING, AND TREE-IN-BUD OPACITIES MOST CONSISTENT WITH INFECTION, INCLUDING MYCOBACTERIAL INFECTION, OR ASPIRATION. UNDERLYING PULMONARY METASTASIS REMAINS DIFFICULT TO EXCLUDE. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION. No pericardial effusion. ABDOMEN / PELVIS: The visualized aorta, celiac artery, superior mesenteric artery, and inferior mesenteric artery are normal in course and caliber. Minimal calcification of the abdominal aorta noted. Mild calcification of the origins of the celiac axis, superior mesenteric and bilateral renal arteries demonstrated without significant stenosis. An accessory left hepatic artery arises from the left gastric artery. Note is made of a small 8-mm splenic artery aneurysm near the hilum. There are single bilateral renal arteries, both normal in course and caliber. The common, external, internal iliac, and common femoral arteries are patent without significant stenosis. Minimal calcification of the common iliac arteries bilaterally. Minimal calcifications are noted in the right coronary artery. The lung bases are clear. The liver and gallbladder are normal in appearance, without evidence of periportal edema or intrahepatic biliary ductal dilation. The pancreas, spleen, adrenal glands, and kidneys are normal in appearance. A 2.6-cm left renal midpole cyst is identified. Both kidneys demonstrate normal excretion of contrast on delayed imaging. No nephrolithiasis or hydronephrosis. The small and large bowel are normal in course and caliber. The appendix is normal. The bladder is mildly thick walled, which may be due to underdistention. The prostate is mildly enlarged and protrudes into the base of the bladder. Two rounded calcifications in the left inguinal canal may represent phleboliths. No ascites or pneumoperitoneum. Small scattered aortocaval and left paraaortic lymph nodes with an prominent aortocaval node measuring 8 mm in short axis. No pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy identified. No suspicious bony lesions identified. Spondylosis of the lower thoracic and lumbar spine. A Schmorl's node is present at the superior endplate of L1 and L4. ENTITY FIELDS

OBSERVATION

An observation is any clinically relevant Finding, Radiological Signs & Signal Alterations, Pathological Conditions & Diseases, Morphological & Structural Abnormalities, Anatomical Status, or Surgical Hardware/Procedure documented by a radiologist.

LOCATION

A "location" represents the explicit Anatomical Site, Organs and Solid Structures, Hollow Organs / Luminal Structures, Anatomical Regions, Subregions / Segments / Lobes, Vascular Structures/ Lymphatic System, Anatomical Structure, Anatomical Level, or Spatial / Directional descriptor.

present: Use when an anatomical site is explicitly described as involved or associated with an entity.

input: FINDINGS: There is bibasilar atelectasis and / or scarring. There is nodular thickening along the left major fissure which may relate to atelectasis. Comparison is limited with prior contrast enhanced study on 4/4 / 2015 given contrast opacification at that time, possibly secondary to a suboptimal bolus. The previously treated segment 8 lesion measures 4.6 x 4.9 cm (04/05/22), previously 3.5 x 4.9 cm on 4-4-2015, and appears more exophytic. The lesion demonstrates internal areas of hyperattenuation on noncontrast imaging consistent with prior chemoembolization. However it also demonstrates areas of arterial hyperenhancement and washout particularly at its inferior aspect. The previously treated segment 5/8 mass demonstrates central hyperattenuation on the noncontrast imaging compatible with prior chemoembolization but demonstrates relatively uniform enhancement over an area measuring 2.5 x 2.7 cm (4/30/25), previously measuring 2.6 x 1.9 cm on noncontrast imaging on 7-26-2038, and demonstrating washout, also suspicious for residual tumor. The most recently radiofrequency ablated lesion in segment 6/7 measures 4.3 x 3.2 cm and demonstrates no hyperenhancement (4/30/2049). There is a 1.7 x 2.4-cm irregularly marginated lesion with a small round focus of hypoattenuation near the junction of segments 2 and 4, not demonstrating definite washout (2022/07/06), previously measuring 1.6 x 1.8 cm on may 12, 2032. There is a 1.5 x 1.3 cm focus of hyperenhancement in the more central portion of segment 8, without definite washout (5/43). Just inferiorly there is a separate more subtle hyperenhancing focus measuring 1.8 x 2.1 cm with washout (May 12/48). There is a 7-mm focus of hyperenhancement peripherally in segment 2, without washout (5/45). There is a small hypodense lesion persistently hypodense on all phases in the left hepatic lobe, too small to characterize (May 2032/49). There is periportal edema. There are paraesophageal and gastric fundal varices. The spleen measures 16 cm in coronal dimension, stable since prior 06/18/21. The pancreas, bilateral adrenal glands and bilateral kidneys within normal limits. There are prominent gastrohepatic and celiac nodes, the latter measuring up to 1.5 cm in short axis, not significantly changed. Bowel and bladder unremarkable. The uterus is prominent and heterogeneous, with likely fibroids. There is trace free fluid in the pelvis. No suspicious osseous lesions. There is an enlarging umbilical fluid collection measuring 3.7 x 2.8 cm, previously 1.9 x 2.3 cm on 32-12-05. IMPRESSION: 1. PREVIOUSLY CHEMOEMBOLIZED LESIONS IN SEGMENT 8 AND NEAR THE JUNCTION OF SEGMENTS 5/8 BOTH DEMONSTRATING AREAS OF ARTERIAL HYPERENHANCEMENT AND WASHOUT COMPATIBLE WITH SOME RESIDUAL VIABLE TUMOR IN BOTH LESIONS. 2. MOST RECENTLY RADIOFREQUENCY ABLATED LESION IN SEGMENT 6/7 MEASURES 4.3 X 3.2 CM AND DEMONSTRATES NO HYPERENHANCEMENT (6/8 / 2039) TO SUGGEST RESIDUAL TUMOR AT THIS TIME. 3. SUBTLE HYPERENHANCING FOCUS MEASURING 1.8 X 2.1 CM WITH WASHOUT (48/6 / 7) IN SEGMENT 8 CONCERNING FOR TUMOR. 4. THE REMAINING FOCAL HEPATIC LESIONS WITHOUT WASHOUT DESCRIBED ABOVE LIKELY REPRESENT ANTERIOPORTAL SHUNTS. 5. CIRRHOSIS AND STIGMATA OF PORTAL HYPERTENSION INCLUDING STABLE SPLENOMEGALY, ESOPHAGEAL AND LIKELY FUNDAL VARICES, AND A PERIUMBILICAL COLLECTION OF ASCITES THAT HAS ENLARGED SINCE 3/18/22.6. PATHOLOGICALLY ENLARGED CELIAC LYMPH NODE, 1.5 CM, STABLE SINCE 3/18/22. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

The tip of the feeding tube is in the third portion of the duodenum. IMPRESSION: 1. input: FINDINGS: Visualized lung bases demonstrate linear scarring and / or atelectasis. There are multiple small nodules in the bilateral lung bases, measuring up to 1 cm, which are unchanged from prior CT and concerning for metastatic disease. No significant pleural or pericardial effusion. There is interval enlargement of the liver compared to 11/05/12, measuring up to 21.8 cm, previously 17.6 cm. There are a few areas of rounded high density, compatible with known melanoma metastatic disease, but diffuse nature of the metastatic disease is better appreciated on prior comparison contrast enhanced CT. Spleen is normal in size and multiple low-density collections are compatible with metastatic lesions. Adrenal glands, pancreas, and kidneys have a normal appearance on noncontrast scan. Urinary bladder is grossly normal. Uterus demonstrates a partially exophytic fibroid arising from the posterior uterine fundus, but is otherwise normal in appearance. No adnexal mass lesions. The stomach and bowel loops are normal in caliber, without evidence of obstruction. There are multiple colonic diverticula, but no CT evidence of acute diverticulitis. Appendix is normal but courses along the right perihepatic space in the right upper quadrant. There is a small amount of free fluid within the pelvis which is stable to slightly increased from prior comparison PET / CT. No free extraluminal gas. Abdominal aorta is normal in caliber with mild atherosclerotic disease. Mildly enlarged lymph nodes in the upper abdomen in the peripancreatic region and porta hepatis measure 1.0 to 1.2 cm in short axis diameter and are nonspecific, but are increased in size from 11/5 / 12'november 5th, 2012 when it measured 7 to 9 mm in. Mildly prominent left para-aortic lymph nodes in the retroperitoneum measuring up to 9 mm are not significantly changed. Previously noted hypermetabolic lymph node in the left pelvic sidewall (4/298) measures 6 mm and is unchanged. Additional mildly prominent left external iliac lymph nodes are also stable. Postsurgical change of the left groin with assistance soft tissue stranding and surgical clips is again noted. Osseous structures demonstrate mild degenerative change of the spine and hips, but no aggressive osseous lesions are identified. Sclerotic focus in the left femoral head is unchanged. IMPRESSION: 1. INCREASING HEPATOMEGALY WITH KNOWN DIFFUSE HEPATIC METASTASES, WHICH ARE BETTER SEEN ON PRIOR COMPARISON SCANS, MAY ACCOUNT FOR THE PALPABLE FINDING IN THE RIGHT UPPER QUADRANT COMMENT ASSOCIATED CAPSULAR STRETCHING MAY ACCOUNT FOR ABDOMINAL PAIN. HEPATIC VASCULATURE CANNOT BE EVALUATED ON THIS NONCONTRAST SCAN, AND IF CLINICALLY WARRANTED, ULTRASOUND CAN BE USED TO FURTHER EVALUATE. 2. SPLENIC AND PULMONARY METASTASES, AS ON PRIOR EXAM. 3. SMALL AMOUNT OF FREE FLUID WITHIN THE PELVIS IS SIMILAR TO SLIGHTLY INCREASED FROM PRIOR PET CT AND IS NONSPECIFIC. 4. COLONIC DIVERTICULOSIS WITHOUT CT EVIDENCE OF ACUTE DIVERTICULITIS. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLY SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

There is no focal consolidation at either lung base, nor is there a discrete nodule or mass at either lung base. The gallbladder is contracted, but normal in appearance, without CT evidence of cholelithiasis or cholecystitis. The spleen is normal in size and appearance. The pancreas is grossly unremarkable, without evidence of a large pancreatic mass, ductal dilatation, or peripancreatic free fluid. Both adrenal glands are normal, without evidence of a discrete nodule or mass. The stomach and duodenum are in normal anatomic positions.
```json
[

    "anchor": "str (field:status)",
    "related": ["str (field:status)"]
  }
]
```

Where:

- "field" is one of: "observation", "location", "degree", "trend", "measurement"
- "status" is: 
  - "present", "absent", or "uncertain" for "observation", "location", "degree", "trend" fields.
  - "current" or "old" for "measurement" field.

Do NOT output anything other than JSON array.

GENERAL RULES

Output must be a valid SINGLE JSON array.
Use only the report text provided.
If the report has Findings and Impression (or Summary, Conclusion) sections, use ONLY FINDINGS section.
If the report lacks Findings, use the given text.
Ignore History, Technique, Dose, Imaging Devices, Administrative Text, and Boilerplate Parts of the report.
Do NOT infer unstated observations, locations, degrees, trends, or measurements; extract only clinical entities explicitly mentioned in the text.
No grammatical, lexical transformation is permitted. DO NOT ALTER WORD FORMS in any way.

ENTITY FIELDS

OBSERVATION

An observation is any clinically relevant Finding, Radiological Signs & Signal Alterations, Pathological Conditions & Diseases, Morphological & Structural Abnormalities, Anatomical Status, or Surgical Hardware/Procedure documented by a radiologist.

Format in graph: "value (observation:status)" where status is "present", "absent", or "uncertain".

COMPOUND OBSERVATIONS
Fixed clinical phrases must be extracted as a single observation entity. Do NOT split them into separate components.

OBSERVATION STATUS

- present: Use when the report clearly confirms the existence of an observation.
- absent: Use when a specific observation is explicitly ruled out or negated.
- uncertain: Use when the report contains equivocal, suggestive, or non-definitive language regarding the observation

LOCATION

A "location" represents the explicit Anatomical Site, Organs and Solid Structures, Hollow Organs / Luminal Structures, Anatomical Regions, Subregions / Segments / Lobes, Vascular Structures/ Lymphatic System, Anatomical Structure, Anatomical Level, or Spatial / Directional descriptor.

Format in graph: "value (location:status)" where status is "present", "absent", or "uncertain"

LOCATION STATUS

present: Use when an anatomical site is explicitly described as involved or associated with an entity.

absent: Use when a specific anatomical structure is explicitly stated to be surgically removed, not visualized, not identified, not seen etc. Do NOT extract surgically removed, not visualized, not identified, not seen phrases as observations. 

uncertain: Use when an anatomical site is not clearly visualized, not well delineated, poorly seen, obscured, partially visualized, indeterminate on imaging etc.

DEGREES

Degree stores qualifiers that characterize the Severity, Extent, Quantity, Distribution, Pattern, Appearance, Morphologic Character, Grade, Relative Laterality Comparison of an observation.

Format in graph: "value (degree:status)" where status is "present", "absent", or "uncertain`.

DEGREE STATUS

present: When the degree term is explicitly and affirmatively stated, and is NOT inside negation or uncertainty scope.

absent: When the degree qualifier itself is explicitly negated.

uncertain: When the degree qualifier is explicitly within the scope of equivocal or hedging language.

TRENDS

Trend stores Stability, Progression, Temporal Status, Temporal Context, Clinical Course / Duration, Recurrent / Re-demonstration descriptors that characterize how an observation or location entity has changed over time.

TREND STATUS

present: Use when a trend descriptor is explicitly stated as present.
absent: Use when a trend descriptor is explicitly negated.
uncertain: Use when a trend descriptor is equivocal or uncertain.

MEASUREMENTS

Format in graph: "value (measurement:status)" where status is "current" or "old`.

MEASUREMENT STATUS

current: Use when the measurement refers to the present study.
old: Use when the measurement refers to a prior study or baseline value.

ABBREVIATIONS

Abbreviations (e.g., sbo, cad, mac, ivh, sdh, edh, sah, cac, ivc) MUST be extracted exactly as written. If an abbreviation represents a pathological finding, clinical sign, or condition, extract it as an Observation. If an abbreviation represents an anatomical structure or region, extract it as a Location. Apply the standard presence/absence/uncertainty logic to the entity type.

NEGATED DESCRIPTORS

Phrases like "not enlarged", "non-calcified" must be extracted without the negation prefix, assigned status = "absent" under the appropriate field.

ANCHOR TYPE RESTRICTION RULE

An anchor node MUST be either: an observation OR a location (only when no observation exists in that sentence context). A trend, degree, or measurement can NEVER be used as an anchor under any circumstance. Trend, degree, or measurement values MUST NEVER be converted into observations.

FINAL INSTRUCTION

Extract medical entities from the given report and return ONLY the relation graph as a JSON array.
input: FINDINGS: The visualized lungs appear clear. Mild bronchiectasis is present within the left greater than right lower lobes. The visualized airways appear patent. Mild bilateral chronic pleural thickening is present posteriorly. The heart appears normal in size. No pericardial effusion is present. Severe 3-vessel coronary atherosclerotic calcification is present. Evaluation of the abdominal viscera is limited by the lack of intravenous contrast. The liver contains a 3.1 x 2.2 cm hypoattenuating lesion within segment 7 of the liver which is grossly similar in size to the prior studies. The gallbladder is surgically absent. There is diffuse fatty filtration of the pancreas. A 1.5 x 1.1-cm fluid attenuation lesion extending from the posterior aspect of the distal body of the pancreas may represent a sidebranch IPMN (series 3 image 114), and is almost certainly of no clinical significance. The spleen again demonstrates focal calcification in its upper pole, possibly posttraumatic or post-infarct change. The adrenal glands appear normal. Right renal cysts are again seen. The left kidney appears normal. There is no renal calculus or hydronephrosis. The urinary bladder is decompressed by a Foley catheter. The abdominal aorta is tortuous with diffuse atherosclerotic calcification. The bilateral common iliac arteries are ectatic. The prostate gland appears normal. No abdominal or pelvic adenopathy is present. No free fluid or free air is present in the abdomen or pelvis. Multilevel degenerative changes of the spine are present, worst at the lower lumbar spine, where there is also severe facet joint arthropathy. Bilateral severe sacroiliac and hip joint degenerative changes are present. Bone islands are present in the right femoral head and left iliac wing. Bilateral fat containing small inguinal hernias are present. There is atrophy of the right upper abdominal rectus muscles with small ventral hernias present in the right upper abdomen; the atrophy is secondary to the Kocher incision made for an open cholecystectomy. The distal esophagus, stomach, small bowel, and colon are normal in caliber. Lipomas are present within the wall of the ascending colon. The colon from the level of the distal transverse colon to the rectum is stool filled. There is mild thickening of the wall of the rectum. No significant infiltration of the perirectal fat is present. No free air or free fluid is present. A 5.5 x 1.8 cm lipoma is present deep to the latissimus dorsi muscle at a level posterior to the right 12th rib. A 2.1 x 1.5 cm lipoma is also present in the left rhomboid major. IMPRESSION: MODERATE FECAL LOADING OF THE DISTAL COLON FROM THE LEVEL OF THE TRANSVERSE COLON TO THE RECTUM, COMPATIBLE WITH CONSTIPATION. THE RECTUM IS STILL DISTENDED AND THERE IS MILD THICKENING OF THE RECTAL WALL. RECOMMEND DISIMPACTION, AS THE PATIENT IS AT RISK FOR STERCORAL ULCERATION. FINAL RESULTS DISCUSSED WITH ER CALL BACK NURSE ON May 1, 2007 AT 4PM. PATIENT HAD BEEN GIVEN GOLIGHTLY IN THE EMERGENCY ROOM WITH SUBSEQUENT BOWEL MOVEMENT RECORDED. PATIENT ALSO REFERRED TO PCP FOR BOWEL REGIMEN. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLY SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

related: posterior aspect of distal body of pancreas (location:present)

input: FINDINGS: There is a small calcified granuloma in the left lower lobe. There is another subcentimeter nodule in the left lower lobe abutting the major fissure. This measures approximately 3 mm in size. The previous study did not include this region. Attention on follow-up. There is no pleural or pericardial effusion. There has been interval development of a 12.1 x 11.2 cm heterogeneously enhancing mass replacing the majority of the right lobe of the liver and demonstrates irregular neo-vascularity on the arterial phase and demonstrates prominent washout on venous and delayed phases of imaging. The mass abuts the liver capsule and there is a small amount of perihepatic fluid adjacent to the right lobe of the liver. The mass is also immediately adjacent to the right hemidiaphragm. Cannot exclude penetration beyond the capsule and involvement of the right hemidiaphragm. This is best appreciated on Series 4, Image 62-71. The posterior segmental branch of the right portal vein is occluded. There are numerous other hypervascular foci scattered throughout the remainder of the liver, which become isoattenuating on the venous phase and do not demonstrate any appreciable washout. These are compatible with arterial portal shunts. There is caudate lobe hypertrophy and prominence of the interlobar fissures; findings compatible with cirrhosis. The liver capsule is not particularly nodular. The pancreas and spleen are unremarkable. The adrenal glands and kidneys are unremarkable. The stomach, small bowel, and large bowel are unremarkable. The appendix is normal. There are no pathologically enlarged lymph nodes in the abdomen or pelvis. There is no ascites. The splenic vein, portal splenic confluence, and main portal vein are patent. There is a replaced right hepatic artery. The hepatic arteries are patent. The abdominal aorta and other major intra-abdominal aortic branches are patent and unremarkable. The urinary bladder and prostate are unremarkable. Mild multilevel degenerative changes are noted in the lumbar spine. There are a few small sclerotic foci in the regional osseous structures, likely representing benign bone islands. IMPRESSION: 1. INTERVAL DEVELOPMENT OF A LARGE MASS REPLACING THE MAJORITY OF THE RIGHT LOBE OF THE LIVER WITH IMAGING FEATURES HIGHLY CHARACTERISTIC OF A HEPATOCELLULAR CARCINOMA. ADDITIONALLY, THERE ARE FINDINGS SUGGESTING THE POSSIBILITY OF CAPSULAR PENETRATION AND POSSIBLE INVOLVEMENT OF THE RIGHT HEMIDIAPHRAGM, AS DESCRIBED. 2. OTHER NUMEROUS HYPERVASCULAR FOCI SCATTERED THROUGHOUT THE LIVER DO NOT DEMONSTRATE ANY APPRECIATED WASHOUT AND LIKELY REPRESENT ARTERIAL PORTAL SHUNT. 3. SMALL 3 MM LEFT LOWER LOBE NODULE, WHICH WAS NOT IMAGED ON THE PREVIOUS EXAMINATION. ATTENTION ON FOLLOW-UP. REDEMONSTRATION OF A SMALL CALCIFIED LEFT LOWER LOBE GRANULOMA. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLY SIGNIFICANT FINDING; MAY NEED ACTION. I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

related: posterior segmental branch of right portal vein (location:present)

input: FINDINGS: There are small bilateral pleural effusions with associated compressive atelectasis. Heavy calcifications of the aortic and mitral valve, as before. Coronary calcifications indicate atherosclerosis. The patient is status post repeat EVAR with placement of a new aorto-uni-iliac endograft. The origin of the new endograft is at mid level of the the superior mesenteric artery origin, which remains patent. There is a small intimal flap seen just proximal to its origin, between the celiac and superior mesenteric artery (series 4, image 83). Status post placement of an occluder device within the right common / external iliac artery. The right common iliac artery is occluded with reconstitution seen at the common femoral artery, at the level of the femoral head. The patient is also status post femoral-femoral arterial bypass. The bypass graft is patent. Although delayed images were not obtained in this examination, there are hyperdensities seen within the inferior aspect of the aneurysmal sac (series 4, image 183), posteriorly, in the region of the prior endoleak seen on delayed images from the 01-19-2013 examination. The source of the endoleak, probably type II, is not seen. However, suspected are small lumbar branch feeders. The aneurysmal sac is stable in size compared to the prior examination, measuring 7.8 x 9.0 cm. There is expected postoperative gas pocket seen within the aneurysmal sac. Again, there is extensive embolic material seen both within and posterior to the aneurysm sac. As before, there is narrowing of the celiac artery origin. As before, there is a replaced hepatic artery, originating from the superior mesenteric artery. Again seen, there is narrowing at the origin of the right renal artery. The left renal artery origin is only mildly narrowed. There is an accessory left renal artery. There is reconstitution of the inferior mesenteric artery seen anterior to the aneurysmal sac. As before, there is aneurysmal dilation seen in the common left femoral artery, measuring up to 1.8 cm. As before, there is a thrombosed left internal iliac artery, similar in appearance. As before, there are hypodensities seen within the liver and kidneys, too small to characterize, but likely representing cysts. There is contrast material seen within the gallbladder. The adrenals, pancreas, and spleen are unremarkable. There is a Foley catheter within the bladder. No evidence for bowel wall thickening or obstruction. No suspicious lytic or blastic bony lesions. There are marked degenerative changes present within the lumbar spine. IMPRESSION: 1. STATUS POST REPEAT EVAR WITH PLACEMENT OF A NEW AORTO-UNI-ILIAC ENDOGRAFT, AS DESCRIBED ABOVE. 2. SMALL FOCAL INTIMAL FLAP SEEN JUST PROXIMAL TO THE ORIGIN OF THE NEW ENDOGRAFT. 3. ALTHOUGH DELAYED IMAGES WERE NOT OBTAINED, EVIDENCE FOR A TYPE II ENDOLEAK IN THE LOCATION OF THE PREVIOUSLY SEEN ENDOLEAK, AS DESCRIBED ABOVE. STABLE SIZE OF THE ANEURYSMAL SAC. 4. RIGHT RENAL ARTERY ORIGIN STENOSIS, AS BEFORE. 5. A 1.8 CM LEFT COMMON FEMORAL ARTERY ANEURYSM, AS BEFORE. 6. SMALL AMOUNT OF FREE INTRAPERITONEAL FLUID NOTED. 6. SMALL BILATERAL PLEURAL EFFUSIONS, WITH COMPRESSIVE ATELECTASIS. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDINGS, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: The lung bases are clear without focal consolidation or pleural effusion. The visualized portion of the heart is normal in size without pericardial effusion. The liver is normal in size and homogeneous in attenuation. There are multiple low density lesions throughout the liver involving all segments of the liver (approximately 30) measuring up to 2.7-cm concerning for metastases. The largest lesion measures 2.7 x 2 cm within segment 8 of the liver adjacent to the IVC. There is no intra-or extrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation. The spleen, gallbladder, and pancreas appear normal. There is thickening of the medial limb of both adrenal glands with no definite nodule identified. The kidneys demonstrate normal symmetric contrast enhancement and excretion. There is no hydroureteronephrosis. Bilateral renal cysts, largest measuring up to 3.2-cm within the right kidney. There is marked abnormal circumferential thickening and enhancement of the cecum / ascending colon measuring approximately 5 x 3.3-cm with luminal narrowing consistent with cecal carcinoma. There are multiple abnormal lymph nodes adjacent to the mass which are predominantly subcentimeter in size. There is a large bilobed lymph node measuring 4.1 x 2.4 cm slightly lateral and anterior to the IVC slightly superior to the aortoiliac bifurcation. No of the enlarged retroperitoneal lymph nodes are identified. There are additional soft tissue nodules within the omentum and omental stranding concerning for peritoneal carcinomatosis. For example, there is a abnormal soft tissue area within the right lower quadrant adjacent to the lateral anterior abdominal wall measuring approximately 2.7 x 1.5 cm (3, 249). Additional soft tissue nodules are noted within the omentum anterior to the transverse colon (series 3, images 220, 150 and 236). There is no ascites. The remaining bowel is normal in caliber without evidence of obstruction or abnormal bowel wall thickening. There is no evidence free air. The urinary bladder and remainder of the pelvic organs are unremarkable. Evaluation of the bones demonstrates no acute abnormality. There are degenerative changes of the spine. IMPRESSION: 1. LARGE CIRCUMFERENTIAL MASS WITHIN THE CECUM / ASCENDING COLON WHICH MEASURES APPROXIMATELY 5 X 3.3-CM WITH SURROUNDING ABNORMAL LYMPH NODES CONCERNING FOR NODAL DISEASE. METASTATIC DISEASE THROUGHOUT THE LIVER MEASURING UP TO 2.7-CM AND EVIDENCE OF PERITONEAL CARCINOMATOSIS. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: Examination is limited by the lack of intravenous contrast. The heart is within normal size limits. No pericardial effusion. A left PICC line terminates at the cavoatrial junction. The distal descending thoracic aorta is normal in course and caliber. The distal esophagus and gastroesophageal junction appear normal. No pleural effusions. Enlarging 1.8 cm spiculated right middle lobe nodule posterior to the fissure is seen on series 3, image 21, previously 4mm on 4/17/13. Other scattered 4 to 5-mm pulmonary nodules appear stable (series 3: image 54, 3:24, 3:27). The liver is enlarged up to 22 cm but has normal contour. No large hepatic mass. No prominent intrahepatic biliary dilatation. The gallbladder appears normal without evidence of radiopaque gallstones, gallbladder wall thickening or pericholecystic fluid. The adrenal glands appear normal. An 8 mm questionable hypodense lesion in the spleen is nonspecific and not seen on prior exams; the possible lesion is only seen on a single slice and may be a traversing vessel. The spleen is enlarged up to 16 cm. The pancreas appears normal. The kidneys are normal in size. No gross renal mass, hydronephrosis or nephrolithiasis. The bladder is collapsed. The uterus appears normal with a focal area of hypoattenuation near the cervix which could represent a nabothian cyst, as was seen on prior from August 29, 2012. The left ovary measures 2.7 cm, similar in size to 2012, August 29th, and contains punctate calcifications in the walls of a cyst. The stomach appears normal. The small bowel appears normal without evidence of bowel wall thickening, pneumatosis or obstruction. Mild submucosal fat deposition is found in the terminal ileum; this nonspecific finding may be due to cytoreductive medications or obese body habitus, and is not likely due to chronic inflammatory bowel disease given lack of any associated inflammatory changes or relevant history. The colon is normal in wall thickness and caliber and contains intraluminal contrast. The appendix is within normal limits. No free fluid within the abdomen or pelvis. The abdominal aorta is normal in course and caliber. No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes within the abdomen or pelvis. No suspicious osseous lesions. IMPRESSION: 1. MULTIPLE RANDOMLY DISTRIBUTED PULMONARY NODULES ARE COMPATIBLE WITH HEMATOGENOUS SPREAD OF INFECTION. THE LARGEST IS A 1.8 CM RIGHT MIDDLE LOBE NODULE POSTERIOR TO THE FISSURE THAT PREVIOUSLY MEASURED 4MM ON 4-17-2013. REMAINING NODULES APPEAR STABLE IN SIZE. 2. HEPATOSPLENOMEGALY. 3. LEFT OVARY MEASURES 2.7 CM, SIMILAR IN SIZE TO 8/29/2012, AND CONTAINS AREAS OF PUNCTATE CALCIFICATIONS IN THE WALL OR SEPTATIONS OF A CYST. RECOMMEND NON-EMERGENT EVALUATION BY PELVIC ULTRASOUND OR MR. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLY SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: Please see the separate report for the CT findings in the chest. The liver is normal in size and smooth in contour. There is diffuse fatty infiltration of the liver. There is a 1.5-cm cyst in the anterior right hepatic lobe, with several additional tiny densities that are too small to characterize, although likely represent additional cysts. There are several calcified gallstones in an otherwise unremarkable gallbladder. No pericholecystic fluid collections are identified. There is lipomatous hypertrophy of the pancreas, particularly involving the pancreatic head. Note is made of punctate calcification in the pancreatic body, likely related to prior pancreatitis. The spleen and adrenal gland appear normal. The kidneys demonstrate normal symmetric contrast enhancement and excretion. There is an 8 mm cyst in the interpolar region of the left kidney. There is no hydroureteronephrosis. The esophagus and stomach are distended and filled with fluid. The bowel is otherwise normal in caliber without evidence of intraluminal obstruction. Note is is mild submucosal edema involving the second portion of the duodenum. Extensive colonic diverticulosis is present without radiologic evidence of acute diverticulitis. There are surgical clips in the right lower quadrant of the abdomen, presumably from prior appendectomy. There is no evidence of free fluid or loculated fluid collections. The urinary bladder and remainder of the pelvic organs are unremarkable. The retroperitoneum and mesentery demonstrate no pathologically enlarged lymph nodes. The bones are diffusely osteopenic. There is an age indeterminant minimally displaced fracture of the posterior left ninth rib and nondisplaced fracture of the posterior left 10th rib. There also age indeterminate mild (less than 25%) compression fractures of the T10 and T12 vertebral bodies. Note is also made of a probable enchondroma in the left femoral head. IMPRESSION: 1. NO EVIDENCE OF TRAUMATIC INTRA-ABDOMINAL INJURY. 2. AGE INDETERMINATE MILD COMPRESSION FRACTURES OF THE T10 AND T12 VERTEBRAL BODIES IN ADDITION TO FRACTURES OF THE LEFT POSTERIOR LEFT 9TH AND 10TH RIBS. 3. DISTENDED FLUID-FILLED STOMACH AND ESOPHAGUS, LIKELY RELATED TO GASTROESOPHAGEAL REFLUX DISEASE. THERE IS ALSO SUGGESTION OF DUODENITIS, WITH MILD SUBMUCOSAL EDEMA INVOLVING THE 2ND PORTION OF THE DUODENUM. 4. CHOLELITHIASIS. 5. PLEASE SEE THE SEPARATE REPORT FOR THE CT FINDINGS IN THE CHEST, ACCESSION # wduNQUvpKWd. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: Small left pleural effusion which is new since the prior study. No pericardial effusion. Patchy areas of atelectasis in the right lower lobe are noted. Note is made of pacer wires in the imaged portion of the heart. Interval increase in size of a retrocrural lymph node, which measures 21 x 17 mm (osiers 2, image 8) compared to 15 x 14 mm previously. More inferiorly, there is another retrocrural lymph node that measures 21 x 17 mm (series 2, image 13) compared to 15 x 14 mm previously. The liver is normal in size and attenuation. At the junction of the right and left hepatic lobes, there is a 9 mm hypodensity (series 2, image 15) which is new since the prior study. No other focal hepatic lesions are identified. No intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary ductal dilation. Note is made of cholelithiasis, without findings to suggest cholecystitis. The spleen, pancreas, and adrenal glands are unremarkable. Note is once again made of bulky mass-like confluent retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy, which is slightly increased when compared to the prior study. Previously demonstrated 4.0 x 3.0 cm left paraaortic lymph node now demonstrates some more mass-like confluent appearance extending to the right of midline, now measuring 8.3 x 5.8 cm (series 2, image 36). Mass-like adenopathy extends along the bilateral iliac chains. There has been interval increase in degree of left-sided hydronephrosis with moderate hydronephrosis noted on the current study. Previously demonstrated left ureteral stent has migrated slightly inferiorly, now with its superior tip in the left ureteropelvic junction. The left ureteral stent is surrounded by mass-like confluent adenopathy as it extends into the pelvis. The stent terminates within a collapsed bladder. There has been interval development of moderate right-sided hydronephrosis since the prior study. No obstructing lesion is identified within the right ureter. The right ureter is surrounded by multiple lymph nodes at the level of the pelvic brim. The distal right ureter is non-dilated. The exact site of obstruction of the right ureter is not clearly identified. There is persistent retroperitoneal stranding. PELVIS: Postsurgical changes of the pelvis are compatible with prior radical prostatectomy. There is extensive sigmoid diverticulosis. In addition, there is mass-like adenopathy in the pelvis, which is not clearly separated from the sigmoid colon (for example series 2, image 73). Findings are suspicious for involvement of the sigmoid colon. The bladder is decompressed. The rectum is within normal limits. There is a moderate amount of simple free fluid in the pelvis (series 2, image 81). Bilateral fat containing inguinal hernias are noted. No enlarged inguinal lymph nodes. Bone windows demonstrate no aggressive appearing lytic or blastic osseous lesions. A porta hepatis node now measures 3.1 x 2.2 cm (series 2, image 24) compared to 1.9 x 1.6 cm previously. A peripancreatic lymph node now measures 3.6 x 1.8 cm (series 2, image 23) compared to 1.7 x 1.3 cm previously. IMPRESSION: 1. INTERVAL INCREASE IN ADENOPATHY WITHIN THE RETROCRURAL, MESENTERIC, RETROPERITONEAL, AND PELVIC REGIONS COMPATIBLE WITH INTERVAL PROGRESSION OF METASTATIC DISEASE. 2. INTERVAL DEVELOPMENT OF MODERATE RIGHT-SIDED HYDRONEPHROSIS AND HYDROURETER, WITH DILATION OF THE PROXIMAL AND MID RIGHT URETER. THE DISTAL RIGHT URETER IS NORMAL IN CALIBER. WHILE THE SITE OF OBSTRUCTION OF THE RIGHT URETER IS NOT DEFINITIVELY IDENTIFIED, THE RIGHT URETER IS SURROUNDED BY LYMPH NODES AT THE LEVEL OF THE PELVIS BRIM, WHICH ARE LIKELY RESPONSIBLE FOR THE OBSTRUCTION. 3. MILD INTERVAL INCREASE IN DEGREE OF LEFT HYDRONEPHROSIS AND HYDROURETER WITH MODERATE LEFT HYDRONEPHROSIS AND HYDROURETER NOW PRESENT. THE LEFT URETERAL STENT CONTINUES TO BE SURROUNDED BY MASS-LIKE CONFLUENT ADENOPATHY. THE PROXIMAL PORTION OF THE STENT HAS MIGRATED INFERFIORLY INTO THE LEFT URETEROPELVIC JUNCTION. 4. INTERVAL INCREASE IN CONFLUENT ADENOPATHY WITHIN THE PELVIS. THE ADENOPATHY IS INSEPARABLE FROM THE SIGMOID COLON, RAISING CONCERN FOR INVOLVEMENT OF THE SIGMOID COLON BY ADENOPATHY. RECOMMEND ORAL AND / OR RECTAL CONTRAST ON FURTHER STUDIES FOR DELINEATION OF THE SMALL AND LARGE BOWEL AND TO ASSESS FOR THEIR INVOLVEMENT BY ADENOPATHY. 5. NEW ILL-DEFINED HYPODENSE HEPATIC LESION WHICH MAY REPRESENT METASTATIC DISEASE, EVALUATION IS LIMITED WITHOUT CONTRAST. 6. STABLE POSTSURGICAL CHANGES COMPATIBLE WITH RADICAL PROSTATECTOMY. 7. INTERVAL DEVELOPMENT OF A SMALL LEFT PLEURAL EFFUSION. 8. CHOLELITHIASIS WITHOUT CHOLECYSTITIS. ENTITY FIELDS

OBSERVATION

An observation is any clinically relevant Finding, Radiological Signs & Signal Alterations, Pathological Conditions & Diseases, Morphological & Structural Abnormalities, Anatomical Status, or Surgical Hardware/Procedure documented by a radiologist.

Format in graph: "value (observation:status)" where status is "present", "absent", or "uncertain".

COMPOUND OBSERVATIONS
Fixed clinical phrases must be extracted as a single observation entity. Do NOT split them into separate components.

OBSERVATION STATUS

- present: Use when the report clearly confirms the existence of an observation.
- absent: Use when a specific observation is explicitly ruled out or negated.
- uncertain: Use when the report contains equivocal, suggestive, or non-definitive language regarding the observation

LOCATION

A "location" represents the explicit Anatomical Site, Organs and Solid Structures, Hollow Organs / Luminal Structures, Anatomical Regions, Subregions / Segments / Lobes, Vascular Structures/ Lymphatic System, Anatomical Structure, Anatomical Level, or Spatial / Directional descriptor.

Format in graph: "value (location:status)" where status is "present", "absent", or "uncertain"

present: Use when an anatomical site is explicitly described as involved or associated with an entity.

absent: Use when a specific anatomical structure is explicitly stated to be surgically removed, not visualized, not identified, not seen etc. Do NOT extract surgically removed, not visualized, not identified, not seen phrases as observations. 

uncertain: Use when an anatomical site is not clearly visualized, not well delineated, poorly seen, obscured, partially visualized, indeterminate on imaging etc.

DEGREES

Degree stores qualifiers that characterize the Severity, Extent, Quantity, Distribution, Pattern, Appearance, Morphologic Character, Grade, Relative Laterality Comparison of an observation.

Format in graph: "value (degree:status)" where status is "present", "absent", or "uncertain`.

present: When the degree term is explicitly and affirmatively stated, and is NOT inside negation or uncertainty scope.

absent: When the degree qualifier itself is explicitly negated.

uncertain: When the degree qualifier is explicitly within the scope of equivocal or hedging language.

TREND STATUS

present: Use when a trend descriptor is explicitly stated as present.
absent: Use when a trend descriptor is explicitly negated.
uncertain: Use when a trend descriptor is equivocal or uncertain.

Format in graph: "value (measurement:status)" where status is "current" or "old`.

MEASUREMENT STATUS

current: Use when the measurement refers to the present study.
old: Use when the measurement refers to a prior study or baseline value.

Abbreviations (e.g., sbo, cad, mac, ivh, sdh, edh, sah, cac, ivc) MUST be extracted exactly as written. input: FINDINGS: The heart is within normal size limits. No pericardial effusion. The distal descending thoracic aorta is normal in course and caliber with dense atherosclerotic calcification. The distal esophagus and gastroesophageal junction are grossly unremarkable. No pleural effusions. Evaluation of the lung parenchyma demonstrates minimal dependent atelectasis. The liver is normal in size, contour and attenuation. No focal hepatic mass. No intrahepatic biliary dilatation. The hepatic and portal veins are patent. Status post cholecystectomy. The adrenal glands are within normal limits. The spleen is normal in size, contour and attenuation. The pancreas is within normal limits without evidence of mass or ductal dilatation. The kidneys are symmetric in size, contour, contrast enhancement and excretion. No focal renal mass, hydronephrosis or nephrolithiasis. The bladder is decompressed with a Foley in place. The stomach is grossly unremarkable. Postoperative changes compatible with right hemicolectomy and an end ileostomy in the right lower quadrant. Multiple dilated loops of small bowel with air-fluid levels measuring up to 4 cm. A transition point is identified in the mid lower abdomen. There is fecalization of the small bowel and pseudo-pneumatosis identified just proximal to the transition point. There is small bowel herniating through a large ventral abdominal wall defect without evidence of incarceration. No free fluid within the abdomen or pelvis. The peritoneum is unremarkable. The abdominal aorta is tortuous with dense atherosclerotic calcification. No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes within the abdomen or pelvis. Dextroscoliosis of the lumbar spine with moderate multilevel degenerative changes. IMPRESSION: 1. HIGH-GRADE SMALL BOWEL OBSTRUCTION WITH A TRANSITION POINT IDENTIFIED IN THE MID LOWER ABDOMEN, LIKELY SECONDARY TO ADHESIONS. THERE IS NO DEFINITE EVIDENCE OF ISCHEMIA. 2. POSTSURGICAL CHANGES STATUS POST RIGHT HEMICOLECTOMY AND END ILEOSTOMY IN THE RIGHT LOWER QUADRANT. 3. EXTENSIVE ATHEROSCLEROTIC DISEASE. THE ABOVE FINDINGS WERE DISCUSSED WITH MD Brooke BY DR. HARLOW SILHA OF RADIOLOGY AT O600 HOURS ON 03/21/2016. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLY SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

There is an approximately 12 cm long segment of small bowel in the left mid to lower abdomen which demonstrates mild wall thickening and mucosal hyperenhancement. There are multiple lymph nodes demonstrated throughout the small bowel mesentery. The largest of these measures 11 mm in the right lower quadrant (301, 39). The vasculature appears normal. input: FINDINGS: There is bilateral lower lobe atelectasis. Otherwise, the lung bases are unremarkable without evidence of focal consolidations nor pleural effusions. There is no cardiomegaly nor pericardial effusion. A mechanical aortic valve is noted. Coronary artery calcifications are present. The distal esophagus is normal. Nonspecific hypoattenuating focus within segment VII of the right hepatic lobe measuring 4-mm (series 3, image 20) too small to characterize, possibly representing a hepatic cyst in the absence of underlying hepatocellular disease. Otherwise, the liver parenchyma is normal without evidence of intra-/ extrahepatic biliary duct dilatation nor focal hepatic lesions. The hepatic and portal veins are patent. There is conventional hepatic arterial anatomy. The gallbladder is unremarkable without evidence of radiopaque gallstones. The pancreatic parenchyma is unremarkable without evidence of ductal dilatation nor focal pancreatic lesions. There is no splenomegaly. The adrenal glands are symmetric bilaterally without evidence of nodularity nor hyperplasia. The kidneys demonstrate normal enhancement and excretion of contrast. The left kidney is mildly atrophic compared to the contralateral side. There is no hydroureteronephrosis. There are multiple hypoattenuating foci within the left kidney (series 3, images 27, 30, 36, 41, and 42) which are too small to characterize, possibly representing small renal cysts. The urinary bladder is unremarkable. The small and large loops of bowel are normal in caliber without evidence of obstruction nor ileus. The appendix is normal. There is no pneumoperitoneum nor free fluid. There is mild pancolonic diverticulosis with a segmental area of circumferential bowel wall thickening and pericolonic inflammatory stranding involving the ascending colon consistent with acute diverticulitis. There is no evidence of intra-abdominal rim enhancing fluid collections to suggest abscess formation. There are no pathologically enlarged retroperitoneal nor mesenteric lymph nodes by CT criteria. There is mild calcified atherosclerotic plaque within the abdominal aorta. The IVC is patent. There is mild multilevel degenerative change of the visualized thoracolumbar spine. No suspicious lytic / blastic osseous lesions are identified. IMPRESSION: 1. MILD PANCOLONIC DIVERTICULOSIS WITH A SEGMENTAL AREA OF CIRCUMFERENTIAL BOWEL WALL THICKENING AND PERICOLONIC INFLAMMATORY STRANDING INVOLVING THE ASCENDING COLON CONSISTENT WITH ACUTE DIVERTICULITIS. GIVEN THE PATIENT'S AGE, MAY CONSIDER FURTHER CHARACTERIZATION WITH INTERVAL COLONOSCOPY AT THE COMPLETION OF ANTIBIOTIC TREATMENT TO EXCLUDE UNDERLYING MASS LESION. 2. NO RADIOGRAPHIC EVIDENCE OF ACUTE APPENDICITIS AS CLINICALLY QUERIED. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: CARDIOVASCULAR: The visualized heart is normal in size without pericardial effusion. Moderate calcified and non-calcified atherosclerotic plaque is present in the abdominal aorta. An infrarenal abdominal aortic aneurysm containing clot measuring up to 50 x 51 mm (series 5, image 202), previously 40 x 48 mm (series 3, image 39), extending to the bifurcation is again seen. The opacified lumen at this level measures 32 x 30 mm, previously 26 x 22 mm. Celiac trunk, SMA, and IMA are patent and normal in course and caliber. Conventional hepatic arterial anatomy is present. Number of renal arteries are as follows: 1 on the right and 1 on the left. No evidence of stenosis or aneurysm. Mild calcified and non-calcified atherosclerotic plaque is present in the iliac arteries. A slight stenosis is identified at the origin of the right internal iliac artery. NON-VASCULAR FINDINGS: The lungs are clear bilaterally without focal consolidations or effusions. Two calcified granulomas are identified in the left base. The airways are patent and normal in course and caliber. ABDOMEN / PELVIS: Evaluation of the abdomen is limited by noncontrast and arterial phases. The liver is normal in size and attenuation with multiple stable left hepatic cysts. Scattered calcifications are consistent with prior granulomatous disease. No obvious focal hepatic mass. No intrahepatic biliary dilatation. Status post interval cholecystectomy. Small fluid collections are seen in the surgical bed, possibly representing bilomas. The common bile duct is normal. The adrenal glands and pancreas are unremarkable. The spleen is normal in size with multiple calcified granulomas. The bladder is mostly decompressed with evidence of trabeculation consistent with bladder outlet obstruction. The prostate is enlarged. The stomach is normal. The bowel demonstrates interval right lower quadrant ileostomy and near total colectomy. The remaining rectum and sigmoid colon demonstrate deposition of submucosal fat, likely representing sequelae of the patient's known history of ulcerative colitis. No free fluid, free intraperitoneal air, or pathologically enlarged lymph nodes are seen in the abdomen or pelvis. MUSCULOSKELETAL: No aggressive osseous lesions. Multilevel degenerative changes of the spine are seen. Addendum Begins *** Preliminary measurements were made during primary interpretation. ADDITIONAL ORTHOGONAL MEASUREMENTS WILL BE PROVIDED BY THE 3-D LAB. 2. INTERVAL NEAR TOTAL COLECTOMY WITH RIGHT LOWER QUADRANT ILEOSTOMY. NO EVIDENCE OF COMPLICATION. 3. INTERVAL CHOLECYSTECTOMY WITH PROBABLE BILOMAS IN THE SURGICAL BED. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLY SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: The visualized portions of the lower thorax demonstrate marked paraseptal emphysematous change with large bullae. The appearance is stable from comparison. A nasogastric tube is coiled in the stomach. There is proximal small bowel dilatation measuring up to 4.9 cm in size. A transition point is noted in the right upper quadrant in the distal jejunum, likely secondary to mechanical obstruction due to adhesions. There is an anastomosis seen in the left upper quadrant with a caliber change beyond the anastomosis. This is likely related to a side-to-side anastomosis rather than an obstruction (transition point is seen in series 3, image 203). The liver is normal in size and contour. There is no biliary dilatation or focal hepatic lesions. The gallbladder appears unremarkable. The pancreas is within normal limits. The spleen is normal in size. The adrenal glands and kidneys are unremarkable with a low-attenuation focus in the left kidney lower pole measuring 8 mm in size, which is too small to accurately characterize. There is no free fluid in the abdomen or pelvis. A normal appendix is identified. Atherosclerotic change is noted in the aorta. Degenerative changes of the spine are seen. Additionally, there are sclerotic serpiginous foci in the bilateral hips in a subchondral location that are suggestive of avascular necrosis. The femoral head contour is preserved. There is no evidence of collapse. IMPRESSION: 1. SMALL BOWEL OBSTRUCTION WITH TRANSITION POINT IN THE RIGHT UPPER QUADRANT LIKELY IN THE DISTAL JEJUNUM. 2. LINEAR SERPIGINOUS SCLEROTIC FOCI IN THE BILATERAL FEMORAL HEADS AS CAN BE SEEN WITH AVASCULAR NECROSIS. NO EVIDENCE OF FEMORAL HEAD COLLAPSE. 3. STABLE PARASEPTAL EMPHYSEMATOUS CHANGES IN THE LUNG BASES BILATERALLY. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDINGS, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.
